followup_notes.changes_from_prior_cycle: Verdict and overall score remain essentially unchanged. I added valid objections from the other reviewers about the quasi-adiabatic/non-adiabatic tension, the GSL order assumptions, the remnant-as-resolution problem, and the long-wavelength/QNM scale tension. I reject the claimed fatal dimensional inconsistency in the core equations after checking the actual PDF formulas.
invalid_points_from_other_reviewers: The claimed fatal dimensional errors in Eqs. (10), (12), and (23) are not supported by the PDF: Eq. (10) has delta r = -r_H dot h_b/(2 f'(r_H)), Eq. (12) contains the dimensionless ratio dot h_b/f'(r_H), and Eq. (23) includes r_H f''(r_H) dot h_b/(8 kappa_0), which has the dimensions of surface gravity.

The blanket claim that the GSL must apply instantaneously to the raw apparent-horizon area is too strong as stated; the sharper objection is that the paper never defines the generalized entropy/horizon prescription under which its secular replacement is legitimate.

The suggestion that the core modulation formulas are probably correct in structure is too generous unless the horizon definition, area formula, and surface-gravity prescription are first made consistent.
valid_points_from_other_reviewers: The quasi-adiabatic calculation and the claim of adiabatic breakdown/non-thermal emission are not reconciled.
The GSL argument needs explicit energy conditions or an appropriate MOG/semiclassical substitute, plus an order-by-order entropy calculation.
The radiation entropy flux being purely second order is not established, especially because Eq. (25) has first-order luminosity corrections.
A damped QNM does not make the first-order term time-average exactly to zero without further assumptions.
A stable zero-temperature remnant storing information is not, by itself, a demonstrated unitary resolution of the information paradox.
The long-wavelength approximation and QNM-imprinting claim require a parameter-regime check.

main_claims.claim: A scalar breathing perturbation produces a time-dependent apparent horizon and area in Schwarzschild-MOG.
main_claims.notes: The Section II calculation gives r_A(t) approximately r_H - r_H dot h_b/(2 f'(r_H)) and A(t) approximately 4 pi r_H^2[1+h_b-dot h_b/f'(r_H)]. Section III replaces this with r_A(t) approximately r_H[1+h_b/2] and A(t) approximately 4 pi r_H^2[1+h_b], and Section IV uses the latter while claiming to use the Section II area.
main_claims.paper_location: Section II, Eqs. (8)-(13); Section III, Eqs. (18)-(19); Section IV, Eq. (26)

main_claims.claim: The effective surface gravity, temperature, and luminosity are modulated by scalar strain velocity and MOG vector charge.
main_claims.notes: The Taylor expansion of f'(r_A) is formally coherent if Eq. (10) is accepted, but the horizon input conflicts with Eq. (18), the dynamical-horizon surface gravity definition is not derived, and the luminosity formula uses the h_b-only area rather than the earlier h_b and dot h_b area.

main_claims.claim: The breathing perturbation h_b is an exact first-order manifestation of an STVG scalar-field perturbation.
main_claims.notes: The paper gives a schematic proportionality between a scalar source and an angular metric perturbation, but does not solve the full linearized STVG constraints, specify gauge, include vector-sector perturbations, or verify the scalar field equation and boundary conditions.

main_claims.claim: The Generalized Second Law is preserved after separating first-order reversible kinematics from second-order irreversible entropy production.
main_claims.notes: The Raychaudhuri argument lacks the needed energy conditions, horizon prescription, boundary conditions, and order-by-order entropy calculation. It also depends on the inconsistent h_b-only area formula and asserts exact time averaging for a damped waveform.

main_claims.claim: MOG resolves the information paradox through transient non-thermal leakage plus a stable zero-temperature remnant.
main_claims.notes: No quantum-field calculation derives a non-thermal spectrum or information flux. The remnant argument treats the MOG charge as an independent fixed charge during evaporation, despite the paper's earlier mass-charge dependence, and a cold remnant storing information is not by itself a unitary resolution.

missing_assumptions.assumption: A precise STVG action, gauge choice, and perturbation truncation under which the pure angular h_b(t,r) ansatz solves all first-order field equations.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Without this, h_b is an imposed metric deformation rather than a demonstrated MOG scalar gravitational wave mode.

missing_assumptions.assumption: A single consistent definition of apparent/trapping horizon and dynamical surface gravity, preferably in regular coordinates.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The main thermodynamic formulas depend on r_A(t), A(t), and kappa(t), but the paper switches between incompatible horizon formulas.

missing_assumptions.assumption: A controlled perturbative regime: |h_b| << 1, |dot h_b|/|f'(r_H)| << 1, wavelength bounds, damping-time bounds, and distance from extremality.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The horizon shift and temperature corrections contain inverse powers of f'(r_H) or kappa_0, so the expansion can fail near the remnant/extremal regime emphasized later.

missing_assumptions.assumption: A two-scale or QFT-in-curved-spacetime criterion separating quasi-adiabatic thermal modulation from genuinely non-adiabatic particle creation.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The paper uses thermal instantaneous formulas for observables while claiming non-thermal emission as a major physical result.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Energy conditions, semiclassical replacements, and boundary conditions appropriate to MOG for the Raychaudhuri/GSL argument.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The claimed positivity of the irreversible entropy production depends on sign control for effective stress-energy and on the horizon law being applied.

missing_assumptions.assumption: An evaporation law specifying how M, M_G, Q_G, and alpha evolve.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The remnant conclusion depends on whether Q_G is fixed, proportional to M, or governed by a running MOG parameter.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Does the full STVG/MOG linearized system admit the pure angular h_b(t,r) perturbation without accompanying h_tt, h_rr, h_tr, scalar-potential, or vector-field perturbations?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: This requires checking the exact STVG field equations, gauge constraints, and scalar/vector perturbation equations beyond the schematic proportionality in the paper.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: What is the correct trapping-horizon condition and Hayward/Kodama surface gravity for metric Eq. (8) in a regular coordinate chart?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The paper's formulas conflict internally, and the result can depend on foliation, null-normal normalization, and the choice of quasi-local surface gravity.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Does the GSL argument close order by order when Eqs. (24)-(25) are used to compute dot S_rad = dot Q/T?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The paper does not provide the expansion; a human expert should verify whether first-order terms cancel or whether the claimed second-order-only entropy flux is inconsistent.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: In Schwarzschild-MOG evaporation, can the system dynamically approach M_G -> Q_G at fixed alpha, or does Q_G scale with M_G so that extremality is not reached?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: This depends on the precise MOG charge convention and evaporation dynamics, neither of which is specified in the paper.

novelty_assessment: The specific combination of Schwarzschild-MOG thermodynamics, scalar breathing perturbations, and quasi-local horizons is potentially incremental novelty. The ingredients themselves are standard: RN-like MOG black holes, apparent/dynamical horizons, Raychaudhuri arguments, and remnant heuristics. The paper's most ambitious claims--non-thermal information leakage, robust GSL preservation, and information-paradox resolution--are not supported by new calculations at the level presented.
score_rationale: The idea is interesting enough to inspect, but the draft is not technically stable: its central horizon/area formulas contradict each other, the STVG perturbation is not shown to solve the field equations, the dynamical surface gravity and GSL arguments are asserted rather than derived, and the information-paradox/remnant claims overreach. Other reviewers added real supporting objections, but the alleged dimensional fatal flaw is not valid. The paper remains in major-flaw territory rather than merely weak evidence.
summary: Follow-up audit leaves my prior judgment essentially unchanged. The paper proposes a dynamical Schwarzschild-MOG toy metric with a scalar breathing perturbation, then claims a velocity-shifted apparent horizon, modulated Hawking temperature, GSL preservation, and a remnant/non-thermal-emission resolution of the information paradox. I do not certify the full proof. Other reviewers surfaced several real concerns, especially the quasi-adiabatic versus non-adiabatic tension and the missing assumptions in the Raychaudhuri/GSL argument. I reject the claimed fatal dimensional inconsistency in Eqs. (10), (12), and (23): in the actual PDF those formulas are dimensionally consistent. The central problem remains internal mathematical inconsistency and unsupported extrapolation from a schematic perturbative ansatz.

technical_objections.evidence: Eq. (10) gives a velocity-driven shift, r_A approximately r_H - r_H dot h_b/(2 f'(r_H)), and Eq. (12) gives A approximately 4 pi r_H^2[1+h_b-dot h_b/f'(r_H)]. Eq. (18) instead gives r_A approximately r_H[1+h_b/2], Eq. (19) gives A approximately 4 pi r_H^2[1+h_b], and Eq. (26) uses S_app approximately S_0[1+h_b]. These cannot all be the same first-order horizon from Eq. (8).
technical_objections.objection: The paper gives contradictory first-order formulas for the apparent horizon, area, and entropy.
technical_objections.paper_location: Section II, Eqs. (10)-(13); Section III, Eqs. (18)-(19); Section IV, Eq. (26)

technical_objections.evidence: The expansion is computed using l^mu=(f(r)^-1,1,0,0) in Schwarzschild-like coordinates, which is singular at f=0. For a metric with areal radius R(t,r)=r sqrt(1+h_b(t)), standard trapping-horizon conditions and null expansions should be derived in regular coordinates; the paper does not show this or discuss normalization/foliation dependence.
technical_objections.objection: The apparent-horizon calculation is coordinate- and prescription-sensitive but is not checked in a regular dynamical-horizon framework.

technical_objections.evidence: Only h_theta theta and h_phi phi are retained. The paper then writes a scalar contribution to delta G_mu nu and concludes an exact correspondence from a proportionality in Eq. (17), without solving tt, tr, rr constraints, fixing gauge, evolving the massive vector field, or matching the QNM ansatz to a scalar wave equation.
technical_objections.objection: The STVG perturbation derivation does not establish that the pure angular breathing ansatz solves the modified field equations.

technical_objections.evidence: The paper takes kappa(t)=f'(r_A)/2 for a time-dependent areal radius. Hayward/Kodama-type definitions generally involve the two-dimensional geometry and derivatives of the areal radius, not just the radial derivative of the static f(r). Eq. (25) also combines the velocity-corrected temperature with the h_b-only area from Eq. (19), not the Section II area Eq. (12).
technical_objections.objection: The dynamical surface gravity and luminosity formulas rest on an assumed static-style prescription.

technical_objections.evidence: The paper assumes the breathing-mode timescale is long enough that local observers measure T_eff(t)=kappa(t)/(2 pi), then inserts this into a Stefan-Boltzmann law. It simultaneously claims adiabatic breakdown and explicit non-thermal Casimir-like particle creation, but gives no Bogoliubov coefficients, stress-tensor expectation value, spectrum, greybody analysis, or two-scale criterion reconciling the two regimes.
technical_objections.objection: The claimed non-thermal particle creation is not derived and is in tension with the quasi-adiabatic thermal calculation actually performed.
technical_objections.paper_location: Abstract; Section III.1, Eqs. (24)-(25); Section V

technical_objections.evidence: Eq. (28) is Raychaudhuri's equation, but the paper infers a positive secular entropy rate without specifying horizon type, boundary conditions, NEC or replacement assumptions in MOG, sign conventions, or how theta^(2) maps to generalized entropy. For an l=0 spherical breathing mode, shear should also be checked rather than assumed. Eq. (25) contains first-order luminosity terms, while Eq. (27) asserts the radiation entropy flux is purely quadratic in dot h_b.
technical_objections.objection: The Raychaudhuri-based GSL argument does not prove positivity of generalized entropy.

technical_objections.evidence: The paper states that M_G and Q_G depend on the mass/deformation parameter, but Section V describes evaporation as approaching an RN-like bound M_G -> Q_G and Fig. 2 treats Q_G as a fixed charge. In the usual fixed-alpha Schwarzschild-MOG parametrization, charge and mass scale together, so evaporation does not automatically drive the solution toward extremality.
technical_objections.objection: The zero-temperature remnant claim lacks a consistent evaporation law for M_G, Q_G, and alpha.
technical_objections.paper_location: Section II around Eq. (2); Section V, Eqs. (30)-(31), Fig. 2

technical_objections.evidence: The derivation assumes lambda much larger than r_H so spatial gradients of h_b can be neglected across the horizon. The information-paradox discussion then says the outgoing flux is imprinted with spacetime quasinormal-mode frequencies. This may be a scale-control issue rather than a strict contradiction, but the paper provides no parameter regime showing both approximations are valid.
technical_objections.objection: The long-wavelength approximation is not reconciled with the later QNM-imprinting claim.
technical_objections.paper_location: Section II before Eq. (7); Section V
